arts.ttl: Tony Mowbray returns to West Brom manager’s job after 15-year absence
arts.sum: Former coach spent three years at club and left in 2009 61-year-old steered Albion to Championship title in 2008 Tony Mowbray will take charge of West Brom for the second time after being appointed head coach on a two-and-a-half-year contract. Mowbray replaces Carlos Corberán, who left the Hawthorns in December to take up the role of manager at Valencia. The 61-year-old steered Albion to the Championship title in 2008 and spent three years at the Midlands club until 2009. Since leaving the Baggies, Mowbray has managed Celtic, Middlesbrough, Coventry, Blackburn, Sunderland and Birmingham. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: West Brom confirm Tony Mowbray contract details and coaching team
arts.sum: Chairman Shilen Patel said: "Tony’s return is driven by the excellent head coach he is now and while I know he is proud of his previous time with the Albion, his desire to achieve new success shone through from our first meeting. West Bromwich Albion have confirmed that Tony Mowbray has returned to the club as the new head coach.

arts.ttl: West Brom injury latest with key Daryl Dike update and Kyle Bartley issue
arts.sum: He is now, finally, able to think about returning to action for the first time in nearly 12 months, which interim boss Chris Brunt confirmed on Friday ahead of the visit of Stoke City on Saturday. "Obviously Kyle Bartley was left out last week as well, just with a niggle, we'll probably give him as much time as we can, probably for the game tomorrow and Tuesday, to see how he is with that.

arts.ttl: Preston and West Brom in talks for same player – Will head to England on Monday
arts.sum: Midfielder Isaac Price is set to leave Belgium and move to the Championship, with Preston North End and West Bromwich Albion negotiating for him. That’s according to journalist Sacha Tavolieri, who reports that Preston or West Brom could secure a deal worth €2-2.5m with Standard Liège. He states Price is set to be involved in The post Preston and West Brom in talks for same player – Will head to England on Monday appeared first on Sport Witness.

arts.ttl: Chris Brunt explains what new West Brom manager will inherit
arts.sum: Brunt considers his mini and unforeseen spell in charge as 'eye opening', having taken the reins over an especially busy period of the campaign; Albion beat Preston North End, but were held by Sheffield United and Swansea City and defeated by Derby County and then, last weekend, Bournemouth in the FA Cup . Albion are expected to re-appoint Tony Mowbray as the club's new boss, almost 16 years after he left the club, following a managerial hunt lasting in excess of three weeks.

arts.ttl: Mark Robins makes demand as Ryan Shawcross joins Stoke City coaching set-up
arts.sum: Robins said: “With Ryan, when you speak to him, he’s always saying he’s eager to learn but what he can’t forget and what he should understand is that he’s got a lot to give and offer. When you’re humble like that you just think you’re you and you’ve got bits of understanding that you might be able to give to other people but people look up to him.

arts.ttl: FFP rules take a hammering as Tom Cannon moves on and Stoke City are left wondering 'what if...'
arts.sum: Tom Cannon is back at Leicester City, his Stoke City loan ended and his 11 goals now no more than a memory to spark warm feelings during what threatens to be a decidedly chilly second half of the season for Mark Robins and Co, and the focus of “what if” recriminations from some Potters fans. For others, a player now tipped to make a £13m move to one of the Championship big-hitters has been condemned as over-priced, not good enough or simply doomed to struggle.

arts.ttl: FA Cup: Chelsea, Man Utd, Liverpool and Tottenham new dates revealed as fourth round TV schedule is announced
arts.sum: Some intriguing ties will take place in the fourth round of the FA Cup

arts.ttl: Stoke City handed transfer advice as 'sensible' Tom Cannon replacement named
arts.sum: Stoke have been tipped to recruit a man Robins signed for Coventry during the summer in Brandon Thomas-Asante, and one pundit and former player reckons it would be a good move for both player and club. Stoke City have been told who to sign to replace Tom Cannon as Mark Robins looks to “consolidate” after taking over as manager at the bet365 Stadium.

arts.ttl: Stoke City hopes shot down as £13m Tom Cannon decision made
arts.sum: Stoke City’s slim hopes of seeing Tom Cannon return to the club for the second half of the season have been sunk by the news that a bid has been submitted - and reportedly accepted - for the Leicester City player. And transfer expert Alan Nixon claims the Foxes have accepted the offer and given permission for club and player to speak.
